title: Keep rollbacks fast and revision-pinned
description: Preserve revisionHistoryLimit and use kubectl rollout undo / helm rollback to a known-good revision instead of hand-editing live objects.
body: Use when a rollout regresses (crashloop, error spike, failed probes) and you must restore service. Do not hand-patch the live Deployment to 'fix forward' under pressure or delete the ReplicaSet history. Actions: identify the last healthy revision with kubectl rollout history; run kubectl rollout undo --to-revision or helm rollback <release> <revision>; verify the restored pods pass probes. Validation signals include rollout status returning to available on the prior revision and the regression signal clearing. Risk boundary: production rollback is a change-controlled action; record the revision and reason and escalate if state (PVCs, migrations) cannot be reverted with the rollout.
